beats.activity: Wake up, coffee with the philodendrons, watch the rain start; Breakfast and check weather; decide on indoor errand instead of outdoor site visit
beats.reasoning: Night owl waking mid-morning; grey drizzly morning suits a slow start. Coffee ritual with houseplants is core to Sage's rhythm.

beats.activity: Drive south toward Tukwila, avoid SR-520 closure by taking I-5 route
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closed 9am–noon; Sage leaves early via I-5 corridor to reach work before closure impacts cross-lake traffic. Takes familiar southern route.

beats.activity: Indoor office work: review native planting specs and permit docs for Volunteer Park project in the rain
beats.reasoning: Rain makes site visits impractical; Sage shifts to desk work—specs, permits, soil reports. Productive use of grey morning; avoids commute stress.

beats.activity: Lunch at a nearby deli; rain is still steady but easing
beats.reasoning: Quick lunch near work; rain persists but Sage is unbothered, just ducks inside. Laid-back approach—no fussy destination.

beats.activity: Walk the Volunteer Park site in lighter rain; check soil moisture and planting zones; Finish site walkthrough; take photos and notes, plan next week's planting timeline
beats.reasoning: Rain eases after 2pm; Sage seizes the window to visit the site and assess conditions. A 30-min drive north from Tukwila; prioritizes hands-on work over desk time.

beats.activity: Commute home to Wedgwood; SR-520 closure has cleared, but take I-5 anyway out of habit
beats.reasoning: Day's rain has passed; Sage heads home early to avoid evening traffic. Familiar I-5 route is predictable. Energy is moderate—site visit was satisfying but not draining.

beats.activity: Rehydrate and change; check on all the houseplants after rain day
beats.reasoning: Sage is a caretaker of living things; after a grey wet day, he checks soil, adjusts watering, greets each plant. Grounding ritual.

beats.activity: Errand: pick up supplies at Ace Hardware and a few groceries at QFC
beats.reasoning: Local, walkable neighborhood errand. Sage runs both hardware and grocery stops in one arc; energy is steady, not high enough for social plans yet.

beats.activity: Simple dinner at home; reflect on the day's work over a quiet meal
beats.reasoning: After a moderate-energy work day, Sage winds down at home. No big social plans; prefers close friends, and today is a solo recharge.

beats.activity: Evening leisure: paint on the porch as the light fades; listen to rain taper off
beats.reasoning: Night owl's prime creative time. Drizzly evening is perfect for brushwork; Sage settles into painting, unbothered by the muted weather. Board game night or friends not planned—solo art is the call.

beats.activity: Wind down, prepare for bed, sleep
beats.reasoning: Night owl's natural late bedtime (10:30pm); 8 hours of sleep carries into early morning. Day ends at home, energy reset for Wednesday.

diary: Woke to the sound of rain drumming on the windows—one of those mornings where the city feels smaller, quieter. I skipped the outdoor site visit and instead spent the early hours reviewing planting specs at the office in Tukwila, which felt productive if a bit claustrophobic. The SR-520 closure had everyone jittery, but I'd already routed around it. Grabbed lunch at the market around noon when the rain was finally easing up. The highlight was walking Volunteer Park in that lighter drizzle—something about wet soil and the smell of it, the native plantings already settling in nicely. Made it home by mid-afternoon, fussed over the philodendrons (they were thirsty despite all that rain outside), then picked up supplies at Ace. Ended the day on the porch with my paints while the light faded and the rain trickled to nothing. There's something grounding about a day like this—indoors and out, all of it necessary.
thought_process: The rain altered my whole approach today. I'd planned to spend more time outside, but the weather made sense of staying indoors for paperwork—no point fighting it. The bridge closure barely affected me since I'd already decided on the I-5 route, though I noticed how quickly the city's rhythms shift when infrastructure fails. The real draw was getting to Volunteer Park in person despite the damp; I needed to see how the soil was responding. By afternoon, my mood had settled into something meditative. The commute home felt unhurried, and I found myself wanting to paint rather than tinker—the kind of shift that happens when you've been moving between indoor and outdoor light all day, and your hands just need something tactile and slow.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
